title: Bounded Node Upload Admission and Quarantine · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs bounded node upload admission and quarantine from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Node upload-admission workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Upload Accessible Feedback
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6138 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users.

Inspect the Upload Accessible Feedback trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Upload Accessible Feedback.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, expose preflight limits, progress, cancellation, validation details, recovery, and non-color status to keyboard and assistive-technology users. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Upload Accessible Feedback, keep Upload Accessible Feedback connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
